history_text: 69-year-old male with a 17-year history of Type 2 diabetes mellitus, currently poorly controlled (HbA1c 10.8%). Comorbidities include peripheral neuropathy. Renal function is mildly to moderately reduced (eGFR 52.4 mL/min/1.73m²). Current medications include Metformin XR 750mg once daily, Insulin glargine 20 units nightly, Aspirin 81mg once daily. No known drug allergies. He denies recent hospitalisations or surgical procedures.
